Phase 5.2 — Advanced Evaluation (Remaining TODO)

The following evaluations are scoped for future phases:

  • Anonymeter: Singling-out risk — probability that an attacker can uniquely identify a real record from the synthetic dataset.
  • Anonymeter: Linkability risk — probability that an attacker can link two synthetic records to the same real individual.
  • Anonymeter: Inference risk — probability that an attacker can infer a sensitive attribute from the synthetic data.
  • Membership inference attacks — determine whether a specific real record was in the training set.
  • Temporal pattern evaluation — inter-visit gap distributions, temporal autocorrelation of lab values, ICD code co-occurrence over time.

Implemented in Phase 5.1: KS Test (Module G), k-Anonymity (Module H), TSTR with RandomForestClassifier (Module I).

Install anonymeter and uncomment the dependency in requirements.txt when singling-out/linkability evaluation is ready.
